title: Could THESE Meteors Still Hit Earth?
summary: This short clip discusses the Taurid meteor stream and its potential danger to Earth. It explains how the stream was formed from a massive comet approximately 20,000 years ago and suggests these meteors could cause significant damage through impacts and air bursts.

events.description: A massive comet entered our inner Solar System's orbit, eventually forming the Taurid meteor stream
The Taurid meteor stream posed a danger to humanity

key_claims.claim: A comet from the Taurid meteor stream hitting Earth could create a big flood
The Taurid meteor stream was formed when a massive comet entered our inner Solar System's orbit around 20,000 years ago
Comet Encke is approximately 1.5 miles wide and is part of the Taurid stream
The southern Taurids cross Earth's orbit yearly from September to November
The northern Taurid streams cross Earth starting in mid to late October
The Taurid stream was a danger to humanity 12,800 years ago and remains a danger today
